title: Result type
instruction:
`Result<T, E>` is either `Ok(value)` or `Err(error)`. Functions return it to signal possible failure.

Run this.
successMessage: Result is an enum: Ok wraps success, Err wraps the error. The caller is forced to handle both cases.

title: match on Result
instruction:
Handle both variants explicitly with match.

Run this.
expectedOutput: Error: invalid digit found in string
successMessage: match on Result is the exhaustive approach. You must handle both Ok and Err. No way to accidentally ignore an error.

title: The ? operator
instruction:
`?` after a Result either unwraps Ok or returns the Err to the caller.

This is the idiomatic way to propagate errors.

Run this.
successMessage: ? is syntax sugar: if Ok, unwrap and continue; if Err, return early with the error. It threads errors through function chains without boilerplate.

title: unwrap and expect
instruction:
`unwrap()` panics on Err. `expect(msg)` panics with a custom message.

Use them in tests or when you KNOW the value is Ok.

Run this.
successMessage: expect is better than unwrap because the message explains why you believe it is safe. If it panics, the message helps diagnose the bug.

title: map and and_then on Result
instruction:
`map` transforms Ok. `and_then` chains fallible operations.

Run this.
successMessage: map transforms Ok values, leaving Err unchanged. and_then chains operations where each might fail.

title: unwrap_or and unwrap_or_else
instruction:
Provide a fallback value instead of panicking.

Run this.
successMessage: unwrap_or gives a default without panicking. unwrap_or_else calls a closure — handy for logging the error before returning the default.

title: Collecting Results
instruction:
When mapping over an iterator and each operation returns Result, collect into `Result<Vec<T>, E>` to fail fast.

Run this.
successMessage: Collecting into Result<Vec<T>,E> stops at the first Err and returns it. All values Ok gives Ok(Vec). Great for parsing user input.

title: panic! for bugs
instruction:
`panic!` is for unrecoverable bugs — index out of bounds, contract violations.

Use Result for expected errors. Use panic for impossible situations.

Run this.
successMessage: Panic is for bugs, not expected errors. A caller passing an out-of-bounds index is a programming mistake, not a recoverable error.
